name: Tokoloshe
description: The Tokoloshe (also known as Tikoloshe, Tokolotsi, or Hili) is a mischievous and malevolent dwarf-like creature from Zulu and Xhosa mythology. It is often described as a hairy, gnome-like being created by a witch doctor to cause harm to enemies. The Tokoloshe is believed to be able to become invisible by swallowing a pebble, and it is said to have only one buttock. It is often associated with nightmares, sleep paralysis, and unexplained misfortunes. Some believe it can cause illness, death, or even sexual assault. To protect themselves, people often raise their beds on bricks or sleep with a brick under their bed. The Tokoloshe is a powerful symbol of fear and the dark side of traditional magic in Southern African folklore, embodying the belief in the power of witchcraft and the vulnerability of individuals to supernatural forces. Stories of the Tokoloshe serve as cautionary tales, reinforcing social norms and highlighting the dangers of malevolent magic and the importance of seeking protection from traditional healers. The creature's enduring presence in popular culture and oral traditions reflects its deep-seated significance in shaping cultural beliefs and practices related to safety, security, and the supernatural.
subtitle: Mischievous dwarf of Zulu folklore
appearance: The Tokoloshe is typically described as a short, hairy dwarf-like creature. Its appearance can vary, but it's often depicted with a large head, protruding eyes, and a single buttock. Some accounts mention animalistic features like a baboon-like face or sharp claws. It is often naked, and its skin color is usually dark.
habitat: The Tokoloshe is believed to inhabit rural areas, often near rivers or in the bush. It can also be found in homes that have been targeted by a witch doctor.
behavior: The Tokoloshe is mischievous and malicious, often causing trouble and harm to humans. It is said to be created by a witch doctor to exact revenge or cause misfortune. It is known for causing nightmares, sleep paralysis, and general feelings of unease. It is also believed to be sexually aggressive and can attack people in their sleep.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the Tokoloshe
keyMyths.description: A witch doctor creates the Tokoloshe by performing dark magic and imbuing it with evil intent. The creature is then sent to terrorize the witch doctor's enemies, causing them misfortune and harm. Some versions state the creature is made from a corpse or clay.

sources.author: Credo Mutwa
sources.description: A collection of Zulu folklore and traditions.
sources.text: A collection of Zulu folklore and traditions.
